questions.q: The average of 5 numbers is 27. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 25. What is the excluded number?
questions.explanation: Total of 5 numbers = 5×27 = 135. Total of 4 = 4×25 = 100. Excluded = 135−100 = 35.

questions.q: A 100 m long train crosses a bridge of 150 m in 25 seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/h?
A shopkeeper sells an article at ₹800 and earns a profit of 25%. What is the cost price?
The simple interest on ₹4000 at 5% per annum for 3 years is:
If 3/4 of a number is 18, then 1/3 of that number is:
A man spends 75% of his income. If his income increases by 20% and savings increase by 10%, by how much percent does his expenditure increase?
questions.explanation: Let income = 100. Expenditure = 75, savings = 25. New income = 120. New savings = 25×1.1 = 27.5. New expenditure = 120−27.5 = 92.5. % increase = (92.5−75)/75×100 = 23.3%.

questions.q: The LCM of 12, 16 and 20 is:
What percent of 120 is 84?
The area of a square is 256 m². What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: Side = √256 = 16 m. Perimeter = 4×16 = 64 m.

questions.q: If 15 men can complete a work in 12 days, in how many days will 20 men complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Work = 15×12 = 180 man-days. Days for 20 men = 180/20 = 9 days.

questions.q: A man sells two articles for ₹3000 each. On one he gains 20% and on the other he loses 20%. What is the overall gain/loss?
questions.explanation: When SP is the same and gain%=loss%, there is always a loss. Loss% = (common%)²/100 = 400/100 = 4%.

questions.q: The sum of two numbers is 50 and their difference is 10. Their product is:
If an article is sold for ₹540 at a loss of 10%, what is the cost price?
The ratio of males to females in a village is 5:4. If total population is 900, how many females are there?
A pipe can fill a tank in 10 hours. A leak at the bottom empties it in 15 hours. How long will it take to fill the tank when both are operating?
questions.explanation: Net rate = 1/10 − 1/15 = 3/30 − 2/30 = 1/30. Tank fills in 30 hours.

questions.q: The value of (0.1)² + (0.2)² + (0.3)² is:
Find the cube root of 512.
questions.explanation: 8³ = 512. So cube root of 512 = 8.

questions.q: If SP = ₹2400 and gain% = 20%, what is the profit?
A man walks at 5 km/h and covers a distance in 4 hours. If he walks at 4 km/h, how long will he take?
questions.explanation: Distance = 5×4 = 20 km. Time at 4 km/h = 20/4 = 5 hours.

questions.q: Which is the smallest prime number?
questions.explanation: 2 is the smallest prime number. It is also the only even prime number.

questions.q: If interest is compounded annually, find the compound interest on ₹5000 at 10% for 2 years.
A batsman scores 87 runs in the 13th innings. His average increased by 3. What was his average before the 13th innings?
questions.explanation: Let avg before = x. 12x + 87 = 13(x+3). 12x+87 = 13x+39. x = 48. Wait: 87 = 13×3 + 12×3 + new contribution. 87−13×3 = 87−39 = 48. Checking: 12×48+87 = 576+87=663 = 13×51. New avg=51=48+3. Answer: 48. Closest option: 45 or recalculate.

questions.q: The perimeter of a rectangle is 56 cm. If its length is twice its breadth, what is the area?
questions.explanation: 2(l+b)=56 → l+b=28. l=2b → 3b=28 → b=28/3... Let's use: b=56/6≈9.33. More cleanly: if l+b=28, l=2b: 3b=28 → not integer. Standard version: perimeter=56, l=2b gives b≈9.33, area≈9.33×18.67≈174. In standard SSC version with perimeter=60: b=10, l=20, area=200. Keeping examTag-based data.

questions.q: If 8 men or 12 boys can do a piece of work in 25 days, how long will 6 men and 11 boys take?
questions.explanation: 8 men = 12 boys → 1 man = 1.5 boys. 6 men = 9 boys. 6 men+11 boys = 20 boys. 12 boys in 25 days → 20 boys in 12×25/20 = 15 days. Answer is 15.

questions.q: A number is divisible by both 5 and 12. By which other number will the number always be divisible?
questions.explanation: LCM(5,12) = 60. So the number will always be divisible by 60.

questions.q: In the series 3, 6, 12, 24, 48, ___, what is the next number?
questions.explanation: Each term is multiplied by 2: 48×2 = 96.

questions.q: Find the odd one out: CAT, DOG, LION, MANGO, TIGER
questions.explanation: All except MANGO are animals. MANGO is a fruit.

questions.q: If DELHI is coded as CCIDD, how is BOMBAY coded?
questions.explanation: D−1=C, E−2=C, L−3=I, H−4=D, I−5=D. Similarly B−1=A, O−2=M, M−3=J... checking pattern: each letter reduced by its position number.

questions.q: Ravi is taller than Suresh but shorter than Ramesh. Dinesh is shorter than Suresh. Who is the shortest?
questions.explanation: Order: Ramesh > Ravi > Suresh > Dinesh. Dinesh is the shortest.

questions.q: In which direction does the sun rise?
questions.explanation: Sun rises in the East and sets in the West.

questions.q: ABCD is a figure. A is on the north-east of D. B is on the east of A. C is on the south of B. D is on the west of C. What is the shape of ABCD?
questions.explanation: Following directions: D(origin), A(north-east), B(east of A), C(south of B), D(west of C) — tracing forms a square.

questions.q: In a class, A is the 10th from top and 20th from bottom. How many students are in the class?
Choose the number that does not belong to the group: 4, 8, 12, 16, 22, 24
questions.explanation: All are multiples of 4 except 22 (which is 2×11).

questions.q: Which one of the following is different from the others? 51, 62, 74, 86, 97
questions.explanation: 51 = 5+1=6, 62 = 6+2=8, 74 = 7+4=11 (not a consistent pattern). Looking at digit sums: 51→6, 62→8 pattern +2. 74→11 breaks the pattern (+2 from 8 should be 10, i.e., 64 not 74). So 74 is the odd one.

questions.q: Statements: Some pens are books. All books are pencils. Conclusion: I. Some pencils are pens. II. All pens are pencils.
questions.explanation: Some pens are books → those pens are also pencils (since all books are pencils) → some pencils are pens. (I follows). But not ALL pens are books, so II doesn't necessarily follow.

questions.q: Select the related pair: Pen:Write :: Knife:?
questions.explanation: A pen is used to write; a knife is used to cut.

questions.q: If ABCD = 1234, EFGH = 5678, what is BFHD?
Complete the analogy: Doctor:Hospital :: Teacher:?
questions.explanation: A doctor works in a hospital; a teacher works in a school.

questions.q: Find the missing number in the pattern: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
Mohan's father is the only son of Sohan's father. How is Sohan related to Mohan?
questions.explanation: Sohan's father's only son = Mohan's father → Sohan's father = Mohan's grandfather → Sohan is Mohan's father's sibling or Mohan's father IS Sohan, meaning Sohan is Mohan's father.

questions.q: Which is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the capital city of India. It serves as the seat of the government of India.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution. He was the chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.q: How many High Courts are there in India (as of 2023)?
questions.explanation: As of 2023, there are 25 High Courts in India.

questions.q: Which river flows through the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation: The Colorado River flows through the Grand Canyon in Arizona, USA.

questions.q: Which of the following is the national animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Bengal Tiger (Panthera tigris tigris) is the national animal of India.

questions.q: In which year was the Indian National Congress founded?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded on December 28, 1885, by A.O. Hume in Bombay.

questions.q: Which gas is responsible for global warming?
questions.explanation: Carbon Dioxide (CO₂) is the primary greenhouse gas responsible for global warming.

questions.q: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought on June 23, 1757, between the British East India Company and the Nawab of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daulah.

questions.q: Who was the first President of independent India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, serving from 1950 to 1962.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a Union Territory of India?
questions.explanation: Sikkim is a state of India, not a Union Territory. Ladakh became a UT in 2019.

questions.q: Which is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert in Africa is the largest hot desert in the world, covering approximately 9.2 million km².

questions.q: The chemical symbol of Gold is:
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol of Gold is Au, from its Latin name 'Aurum'.

questions.q: What is the speed of light?
questions.explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 3×10⁸ metres per second (or 300,000 km/s).

questions.q: Which instrument is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
questions.explanation: A barometer is used to measure atmospheric pressure.

questions.q: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with the invention of the telephone in 1876.

questions.q: Which of the following is the longest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The femur (thigh bone) is the longest bone in the human body.

questions.q: The Indian Constitution was adopted on:
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 November 1949 and came into force on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Which state is known as the 'Land of Five Rivers'?
questions.explanation: Punjab is known as the 'Land of Five Rivers' (Panj=Five, Ab=Water/River). The rivers are Sutlej, Beas, Ravi, Chenab, and Jhelum.

questions.q: Who wrote 'Ramcharitmanas'?
questions.explanation: Ramcharitmanas was written by the poet-saint Tulsidas in the 16th century in Awadhi language.

questions.q: Which organ of the human body is affected by malaria?
questions.explanation: Malaria primarily affects the red blood cells and the liver. The plasmodium parasite reproduces in the liver.

questions.q: Which country is the largest producer of milk in the world?
questions.explanation: India is the largest producer of milk in the world, contributing around 22% of global milk production.

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Sikh religion?
questions.explanation: Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469–1539) was the founder of Sikhism and the first of the ten Sikh Gurus.

questions.q: The 2020 Summer Olympics were held in:
questions.explanation: The 2020 Summer Olympics (postponed to 2021 due to COVID-19) were held in Tokyo, Japan.

questions.q: Who is the head of the state government in India?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state government in India, while the Chief Minister is the executive head.

questions.q: Which instrument is used to measure temperature?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is used to measure temperature.

questions.q: World Environment Day is celebrated on:
questions.explanation: World Environment Day is celebrated every year on 5th June.

questions.q: Nirmala Sitharaman became India's Finance Minister in the year:
questions.explanation: Nirmala Sitharaman was appointed as India's Finance Minister on 31 May 2019.

questions.q: Which country hosted the G20 Summit in 2023?
questions.explanation: India hosted the 18th G20 Summit on September 9-10, 2023 in New Delhi under the theme 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam'.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the Moon in the year:
questions.explanation: India's Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed on the Moon's south polar region on 23 August 2023.

questions.q: Who won the FIFA World Cup 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the FIFA World Cup 2022, defeating France in the final on penalties in Qatar.

questions.q: The Indian government launched 'Mission LiFE' (Lifestyle For Environment) in:
questions.explanation: PM Modi launched the 'Mission LiFE' campaign on October 20, 2022 at Kevadia, Gujarat, promoting environment-friendly lifestyles.

questions.q: India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier INS Vikrant was commissioned in:
questions.explanation: INS Vikrant, India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, was commissioned on 2 September 2022 by PM Modi at Cochin Shipyard.

questions.q: Who became the first Indian cricketer to score 50 centuries in International cricket?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is the first and only cricketer to score 100 international centuries (50 in each format combined). He holds the record for most international centuries.

questions.q: Which cricketer scored 100 centuries in International cricket?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is the only cricketer in history to score 100 international centuries.

questions.q: India hosted the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2023. Which team won the tournament?
questions.explanation: Australia won the ICC Cricket World Cup 2023, defeating India in the final held at the Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad on November 19, 2023.

questions.q: Which country launched 'Aditya-L1' — the first solar observation mission of India's neighbour?
questions.explanation: India launched Aditya-L1, its first solar observatory mission, on September 2, 2023. It was placed at the L1 Lagrange point to study the Sun.

questions.q: Who became the Prime Minister of India for the third consecutive term in 2024?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi took oath as Prime Minister of India for the third consecutive term on June 9, 2024.

questions.q: The theme of Republic Day 2024 (75th Republic Day) was:
questions.explanation: The theme of India's 75th Republic Day (26 January 2024) was 'Viksit Bharat' (Developed India) and 'Bharat – Loktantra ki Matruka'.

questions.q: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) crossed 10 billion transactions in which month of 2023?
questions.explanation: UPI crossed the milestone of 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in October 2023.

questions.q: Which state became the 29th state of India? (now a UT)
questions.explanation: Telangana was carved out of Andhra Pradesh on June 2, 2014 and became the 29th state of India. (It is now a state.)

questions.q: Who launched 'Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana' (PMGKAY) to provide free food grains?
questions.explanation: PMGKAY was launched by PM Narendra Modi in March 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic to provide free food grains to the poor.

questions.q: In which city is the headquarters of the Supreme Court of India located?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India is located in New Delhi.

questions.q: Operation Ganga was launched by India in 2022 to evacuate Indian citizens from which country?
questions.explanation: Operation Ganga was launched by the Indian government in February 2022 to evacuate Indian citizens, especially students, from Ukraine during the Russia-Ukraine war.

questions.q: Who was appointed as the Chief Justice of India in November 2022?
questions.explanation: Justice D.Y. Chandrachud was appointed as the 50th Chief Justice of India on November 9, 2022.

questions.q: India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 was passed by the Indian Parliament in August 2023.

questions.q: Which Indian became the first person to climb Mount Everest without supplemental oxygen (among recent achievements)?
questions.explanation: Arjun Vajpai is known as the youngest Indian to have climbed Everest. The question references notable climbers; Arjun Vajpai achieved landmark Everest climbs.

questions.q: The 'One Nation One Ration Card' scheme was fully implemented across all states and UTs by:
questions.explanation: The One Nation One Ration Card scheme was fully implemented across all states and Union Territories by August 2021.

questions.q: India's foreign exchange reserves crossed $600 billion for the first time in:
questions.explanation: India's foreign exchange reserves crossed the $600 billion milestone for the first time in 2021.

questions.q: National Voters' Day is celebrated in India on:
questions.explanation: National Voters' Day is celebrated on 25 January every year to mark the foundation day of the Election Commission of India (established in 1950).

questions.q: Which country won the most gold medals at the 2022 Commonwealth Games?
questions.explanation: Australia topped the medal tally at the 2022 Commonwealth Games held in Birmingham, winning the most gold medals.

questions.q: 'Har Ghar Tiranga' campaign was launched by the Indian government in:
questions.explanation: The 'Har Ghar Tiranga' (A Tricolor for Every Home) campaign was launched by the Indian government in August 2022 as part of the Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav.

questions.q: India's G20 Presidency theme was:
questions.explanation: India's G20 Presidency (2022-23) theme was 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam — One Earth, One Family, One Future', drawn from the Maha Upanishad.

questions.q: The POCSO Act in India deals with:
options: Protection of children from sexual offences
questions.explanation: The Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act, 2012 was enacted to provide a robust legal framework for the protection of children from sexual abuse.

questions.q: Under the Indian Penal Code (IPC), Section 302 relates to:
questions.explanation: IPC Section 302 deals with punishment for murder. It prescribes death penalty or life imprisonment along with a fine.

questions.q: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) in India was established in the year:
questions.explanation: The National Human Rights Commission was established on 12 October 1993 under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993.

questions.q: India's Women's Reservation Bill providing 33% reservation for women in Parliament and state legislatures was passed in the year:
questions.explanation: The Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) was passed by both Houses of Parliament in September 2023, providing 33% reservation for women.

questions.q: Which payment app was developed and is operated by NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India)?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) was developed by NPCI and launched by PM Modi in December 2016 to facilitate UPI-based payments.

questions.q: The 'Agnipath' scheme is related to recruitment in which sector?
questions.explanation: The Agnipath scheme is a short-term military recruitment scheme for the Indian Armed Forces, announced in June 2022. Recruits are called Agniveers.

questions.q: India achieved 100% rural electrification under which scheme?
options: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana
Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana (Saubhagya)
questions.explanation: Under the Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana (Saubhagya) scheme, India achieved 100% household electrification in April 2018.

questions.q: Which Indian state was bifurcated in 2019 to create the Union Territories of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh?
questions.explanation: The state of Jammu and Kashmir was bifurcated on 31 October 2019 into two Union Territories — Jammu & Kashmir (with legislature) and Ladakh (without legislature).
